CONTENT.Career: Riznyk is a product of FC Vorskla Youth Sportive School from his native Poltava. In July 2016 he was promoted to the Vorskla main squad to compete in the Ukrainian Premier League Reserves. And in September 2019 Riznyk was promoted to the main-squad team of FC Vorskla in the Ukrainian Premier League.He made his debut as a start-squad player for Vorskla Poltava in the Ukrainian Premier League in a losing match against FC Zorya Luhansk on 26 October 2019.On 10 February 2023, Shakhtar Donetsk announced the signing of Riznyk on a five-year contract.
CONTENT.Honours: Vorskla PoltavaUkrainian Cup runner-up: 2019–20Ukraine U20FIFA U-20 World Cup: 2019
CONTENT.External links: Dmytro Riznyk at SoccerwayDmytro Riznyk at UAF and archived FFU page (in Ukrainian)
CONTENT.International career: From 2011, until 2012, Riznyk has been part of Ukraine at youth international level, respectively has been part of the U19. U20 and U21 teams and he with these teams played four matches. He was a part of the Ukraine U20 that won the 2019 FIFA U-20 World Cup, even if he not played any game and spent a time on the bench.He made his debut for Ukraine national football team on 11 November 2021 in a friendly against Bulgaria.

SUMMARY: Dmytro Hryhorovych Riznyk (Ukrainian: Дмитро Григорович Різник; born 30 January 1999) is a Ukrainian professional footballer who plays as a goalkeeper for Shakhtar Donetsk in the Ukrainian Premier League.
